verdicts.mechanism: Line 77's `if not (major == 1): assert minor >= 21` inverts upstream's `if major == 1:` guard, so urllib3 1.20 (major==1) skips the minor check and passes while urllib3 2.0.7 (major==2, minor=0) trips the assert and emits a spurious RequestsDependencyWarning via the except at line 118.

is_prepared does unconditionally return True at runtime (lines 49-52), but the explicit comment 'noop at runtime to avoid AssertionError' shows this is a deliberate static-only TypeIs narrowing, and no shown caller relies on it for runtime validation, so no concrete triggerable defect is demonstrated.

The `except MaxRetryError` block ends with `pass` (line 729) instead of `raise ConnectionError(e, request=request)`, so a retry-exhaustion whose reason matches none of the isinstance checks (e.g. NewConnectionError from a refused connection, explicitly excluded at line 716) is swallowed and control reaches `build_response(request, resp)` at line 748 with `resp` never assigned, raising UnboundLocal

Line 738's `if not (isinstance(e, _SSLError)): raise SSLError(e, ...)` makes every non-SSL _HTTPError (including ReadTimeoutError) raise as SSLError before line 741's elif can run, while a true _SSLError skips it and hits the bare `raise` at line 746, re-raising the raw urllib3 exception instead of requests.SSLError.

Line 18's `if not (TYPE_CHECKING):` makes the `typing_extensions`/`_types` imports run at runtime (ImportError if typing_extensions isn't installed, which requests doesn't require) while type checkers — for whom TYPE_CHECKING is True — skip the branch and see `Unpack`/`_t` as undefined names in every signature, the exact inverse of the intended `if TYPE_CHECKING:` guard.

Line 291 uses r.headers.get("www-authenticate") with no default, so any 4xx response lacking that header (e.g. a plain 403/404, since the only guard at line 282 is the status range) makes s_auth None and line 293's s_auth.lower() raises AttributeError inside the response hook instead of returning r.

At runtime TYPE_CHECKING is False, so `if not (TYPE_CHECKING)` executes the bare `import chardet` (line 49), which raises ImportError when only charset_normalizer is installed, while the `_resolve_char_detection()` fallback on line 51 is confined to the static-analysis branch.

Line 87 does return `self._r.headers.get(name)` without passing `default`, so the parameter is genuinely dead — but `http.cookiejar` (the only consumer of this shim) never calls get_header with a default it relies on, so no observable misbehavior is triggerable; it's a latent interface-fidelity flaw.

Line 132 does discard the result of `self._headers.getheaders(name)`, but on Python 3 `http.cookiejar.extract_cookies` only calls `response.info()` and then `get_all()` on the message (and Py3 HTTPMessage has no `.getheaders`, so the body would AttributeError if ever invoked) — the method is dead Py2-compat code with no live caller, making this a latent flaw rather than a triggerable defect.

Line 516 `badargs = (set(kwargs)) + (set(result))` applies `+` to two sets, which raises TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) on every create_cookie() call (even with valid kwargs), whereas the original requests code uses set difference `set(kwargs) - set(result)`.

The `except ValueError: pass` at lines 538-539 does swallow a malformed max-age leaving expires=None, but treating an unparseable Max-Age as absent matches RFC 6265 §5.2.2 (ignore invalid max-age) — a lenient design choice, not a triggerable correctness defect.

With no kwargs, `response` is None so the inner conjunction is False, `not (...)` is True, and line 34 executes `self.request = response.request` raising AttributeError on None — the guard is inverted versus upstream requests' `if response is not None and not self.request and hasattr(response, 'request')`.

Line 93 `if not (OpenSSL):` enters the block exactly when OpenSSL is None (import failed), so line 95 `OpenSSL.__version__` raises AttributeError on None, and when the module imported successfully the truthy module skips the block, leaving pyopenssl_info unpopulated — the condition should be `if OpenSSL:`.

Line 1017 computes `(pending) - (chunk)` where both operands are str/bytes, and `-` is unsupported for those types, so any iteration where a partial line carries over (pending set at line 1025) raises TypeError instead of concatenating.

Line 1074 `if not (self.encoding is None)` overwrites an explicitly-set `self.encoding` with `apparent_encoding` (line 1075), while a None encoding skips detection and silently falls back to `encoding or "utf-8"` at line 1079 — the exact inversion of the intended `if self.encoding is None` fallback.

Line 19's `if not (mod == target or mod.startswith(f"{target}."))` inverts the intended filter (upstream requests has no `not`), so the loop skips chardet/charset_normalizer modules — `requests.packages.chardet.*` is never created — while aliasing every unrelated sys.modules entry under `requests.packages.*`.

Line 324's `if not ("Authorization" in headers and self.should_strip_auth(...))` keeps the header exactly when both conditions demand stripping (cross-host credential leak) and executes `del headers["Authorization"]` (line 327) whenever the header is absent, raising KeyError on every ordinary redirect.

Line 787 reads `elapsed = (preferred_clock()) + (start)`, summing two absolute clock timestamps instead of subtracting, so `r.elapsed` at line 788 becomes a timedelta of roughly twice the clock's absolute value rather than the request duration.
Line 122 computes `(__doc__ + "\n") - ("\n".join(...))` and since the module docstring is non-None and `_init()` runs at line 128 on import, the str `-` str operation raises TypeError at import time.

Lines 55-56 read `if not (data is None): data = {}`, which overwrites any supplied data with an empty dict (silently dropping all constructor items, e.g. CaseInsensitiveDict({'A':1}) ends up empty and breaks copy()/__eq__ at lines 82/90), while data=None falls through to self.update(None) where MutableMapping.update iterates None and raises TypeError.

Line 130 `return self.__dict__.get(key)` drops the `default` parameter entirely, so `d.get('missing', fallback)` returns None instead of fallback — a concrete, triggerable correctness defect contradicting the declared overloads.

The `except BaseException:` block at lines 336-338 ends with `pass` and no `raise`, and because atomic_open is a @contextlib.contextmanager generator, swallowing the exception at the yield suppresses it for the caller too — the with-block exits cleanly with `filename` never written (os.replace on line 335 skipped), silently losing the write (and even swallowing KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit).

Line 564 `value = param[(idx) - (1):]` slices from one character before '=' instead of one after, so 'charset=utf-8' yields value 't=utf-8' (idx=7, param[6:]), which get_encoding_from_headers then returns as a bogus encoding.
